purpose: A reusable input validator that accepts a schema definition and validates incoming data objects against it, returning structured error messages. Used to enforce data integrity at API boundaries.

endpoints.description: Validate a JSON payload against a schema

sidecar_specification.purpose: Input validation middleware for API endpoints

agent_usage.when_to_use: Before processing any untrusted input, especially user-facing APIs
agent_usage.when_not_to_use: When input is already validated upstream or fully trusted

failure_modes.recovery: Return 400 with error details to caller

agent_notes.implementation_hint: Use Zod for schema definitions. The /validate endpoint should accept { schemaName, payload } and return { valid: boolean, errors: string[] }. Do NOT use ajv or joi — Zod only.
agent_notes.known_gotchas: Zod refinements can throw confusing errors. Always use safeParse and map issues to flat strings.
agent_notes.preferred_approach: Create a SchemaRegistry class that loads schemas from a local directory. Cache parsed schemas.
agent_notes.avoid_approach: Do NOT inline schemas in route handlers. Do NOT mutate the input payload during validation.
